_comment_platform: apiRuntime tells SWA which Azure Functions runtime to provision for the managed function in clearai-frontend/api/. Required — without it the deployer fails with 'Function language info isn't provided.' Node 20 is the highest currently-supported runtime per Microsoft Learn's supported-languages-and-runtimes table (Node 22 not yet listed). Bump when SWA adds Node 22.
_comment_navigationFallback: SPA fallback — unknown paths serve index.html so client-side routing handles them. Excludes asset paths AND recon paths so /.env, /.git/*, /admin etc. return real 404s instead of the SPA shell (frontend security review L2). /api/* must be excluded so SWA managed-functions get the request, not the rewrite.
_comment_responseOverrides: Real status codes for known not-found paths. Without this they'd return 200 + index.html (frontend L2).
_comment_globalHeaders: Defence-in-depth headers applied to every response. CSP is enforced via this HTTP header; Astro emits a small set of inline <script> blocks (lang detection in <head>, Astro hydration loader, astro-island custom element) so we pin each one with its SHA-256 hash rather than allowing 'unsafe-inline'. Rationale per finding (frontend security review): H2 CSP, M1 X-Frame-Options + frame-ancestors, M2 Permissions-Policy, L1 HSTS extended to 1y, I2 X-XSS-Protection removed (defaults to off when absent — we explicitly set 0 to override the previous infrastructure default).
_comment_csp_inline_hashes: SHA-256 hashes of every inline <script> we ship. These are deterministic per build — Astro re-emits the same bytes — so the hashes are stable as long as we don't edit the inline blocks in src/layouts/Layout.astro or upgrade Astro across a major. When the browser console reports a new hash, paste it here. Hash 1 = the cookie/?lang detection IIFE in <head>. Hash 2 = the Astro hydration loader (window.Astro.load). Hash 3 = the astro-island custom-element definition.
_comment_csp_msal_apim: connect-src + frame-src + form-action allowlist Microsoft's OAuth endpoint (https://login.microsoftonline.com — MSAL token POSTs + the silent-refresh hidden iframe + the auth-code form-back) and our APIM gateway as the EXACT origin (no *.azure-api.net wildcard — tightest possible attack surface). frame-ancestors 'none' + X-Frame-Options: DENY remain unchanged: those control whether the SPA can be framed (clickjacking defence). frame-src is the inverse direction — it lets the SPA frame Microsoft for silent-refresh. font-src includes data: because @fontsource inlines small font subsets as base64 data URIs in the bundled CSS.

globalHeaders.Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'sha256-iW2NyQ3LqcIsrp9vO7cGVrl5qe2Y9EpFAN8inym8v2A=' 'sha256-QzWFZi+FLIx23tnm9SBU4aEgx4x8DsuASP07mfqol/c=' 'sha256-BrDhGE1lwa85arfXcrBxSo+n37uVSX5CAROXnIM6Q+g='; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; font-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self' https://login.microsoftonline.com https://apim-infp-clearai-be-dev-gwc-01.azure-api.net; frame-src https://login.microsoftonline.com; frame-ancestors 'none'; form-action 'self' https://login.microsoftonline.com; base-uri 'self'; object-src 'none'; upgrade-insecure-requests
globalHeaders.Permissions-Policy: accelerometer=(), ambient-light-sensor=(), autoplay=(), battery=(), camera=(), display-capture=(), document-domain=(), encrypted-media=(), execution-while-not-rendered=(), execution-while-out-of-viewport=(), fullscreen=(self), geolocation=(), gyroscope=(), keyboard-map=(), magnetometer=(), microphone=(), midi=(), navigation-override=(), payment=(), picture-in-picture=(), publickey-credentials-get=(), screen-wake-lock=(), sync-xhr=(), usb=(), web-share=(), xr-spatial-tracking=(), interest-cohort=()

_comment_routes: Force Cache-Control on the API path so SWA's edge never caches a per-user (future) or per-request (today) BFF response. The bundle assets at /_astro/* are immutably hashed by Astro; SWA's default Cache-Control: public,max-age=300,must-revalidate is fine for them.
